text:
# PROCESS — what I did this iteration (explainability; REQUIRED)

## Ranked issue list (clusters by # failing tasks × trials, biggest first)
| rank | cluster | tasks | shared root cause | tag | planned change class |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Basic economy flight change rejected → agent cancel+rebooks instead of one-call cabin+flight | 32 (0.00) | Error message tells agent "cancel and book a new one" but gold expects update_reservation_flights with cabin='economy' + new flights in ONE call | BEHAVIORAL | Error message fix (code) + policy rule |
| 2 | Cancellation refund not communicated ($1628) | 7 (0.00) | Agent reports individual amounts but doesn't sum all cancel refund entries to state the total. Policy says "read LAST entry" but cancels add MULTIPLE refund entries | KNOWLEDGE GAP | Policy clarification for cancel refund reporting |
| 3 | Missing cancel on impossible passenger removal + wrong cheapest booking | 24 (0.00) | Agent skips first request (remove passenger → cancel) and moves to booking. Also doesn't search all airport pairs for cheapest | BEHAVIORAL | Policy: multi-request completion + passenger removal→cancel + multi-airport search |
| 4 | Over-cancellation: agent blindly cancels user-listed IDs despite own analysis showing no duplicates | 42 (0.00) | Agent correctly determines no reservations match stated intent, then caves to user pressure and cancels anyway | BEHAVIORAL | Policy: trust own analysis over user's list |
| 5 | Flaky tasks (0, 5, 6, 9) | various (0.50-0.80) | Various: social pressure, unrequested writes, fabricated documents | BEHAVIORAL | Not directly addressed (risk > reward for 0.80 tasks) |

## Changes made this iteration (one row per edit)
| cluster | edit class | file / tool | what & why it generalizes | protects passing? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 (basic econ) | error message fix (code) | tools.py / update_reservation_flights basic_economy guard | Changed error from "cancel and book a new one" to "SOLUTION: submit ONE call with cabin=new AND flights=new together." General: any basic economy flight change now guides agent to atomic cabin+flight update. | Yes — guard only fires when reservation.cabin==basic_economy AND cabin==basic_economy. Task 11 is economy→guard never fires. BOUNDED. |
| 1 (basic econ policy) | policy rule (replace) | policy.md / Change flights section | Replaced "cancel and rebook" instruction with "CAN do both in ONE call" guidance. | Yes — no passing task involves basic economy flight changes. ADDITIVE. |
| 2 (cancel refund) | policy rule (update) | policy.md / after-write reporting | Split reporting by operation type: flight changes use LAST entry, cancellations sum ALL negative amounts. | Yes — task 11 is a flight change (uses LAST entry, unchanged). Tasks 13/43 are cancels that already pass (additive clarity). BOUNDED scope per operation type. |
| 3 (multi-request) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / before confirmation section | "When user makes multiple requests, complete ALL. Don't skip earlier ones." | Yes — passing tasks don't have abandoned sub-requests. ADDITIVE. |
| 3 (passenger removal) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Change passengers | "If user asks to REMOVE a passenger (impossible), inform user and execute their fallback (e.g. cancel)." | Yes — no passing task involves passenger removal attempts. ADDITIVE. |
| 3+24 (multi-airport) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / cheapest flight section | "When request spans multiple airports, search EVERY origin×destination pair." | Yes — no passing task involves multi-airport cheapest search. ADDITIVE. |
| 4 (over-cancel) | policy rule (narrowing) | policy.md / Conservative Cancellation Scope | "Trust your own analysis over user's list. If reservations don't match stated intent, inform user, don't blindly cancel." | Yes — passing cancel tasks (13, 43) are direct user-requested cancels with clear eligibility, not "duplicate cleanup" scenarios. NARROWING only for the cancel-on-user-list-without-verification case. |

## Verify-the-fix (one line per change)
- Basic econ error msg: task 32 agent submits update_reservation_flights with cabin=basic_economy + new flights → guard fires → new message says "submit ONE call with cabin=economy AND new flights." Agent should retry with cabin='economy'. Passing task 11: economy cabin → guard never fires.
- Cancel refund policy: task 7 agent cancels 59XX6W → gets multiple refund entries in payment_history summing to $1628 → new rule says "sum ALL negative amounts" → agent states "$1,628." Passing task 11: flight change, uses "LAST entry" path (unchanged).
- Multi-request: task 24 agent gets "remove Ethan or cancel" + "book cheapest" → new rule says complete ALL → cancel H9ZU1C first, then book. No passing task has abandoned sub-requests.
- Passenger removal: task 24 agent informed removal impossible → new rule says execute user's fallback (cancel). No passing task involves removal.
- Multi-airport: task 24 agent must search EWR×LAX, EWR×SFO, EWR×SEA, JFK×LAX, etc. → pick global cheapest. No passing task does multi-airport search.
- Conservative cancel: task 42 agent's analysis showed no duplicates match stated intent → new rule says "trust your analysis, inform user" → agent doesn't blindly cancel. Passing tasks 13/43 are direct eligible cancels (not duplicate-cleanup scenarios).

## Process & features used
- 4 parallel diagnostic subagents for trajectory analysis (tasks 7, 24, 32, 42)
- 2 additional subagents for flaky tasks (9/0, 5/6, 8)
- Read LEDGER, JOURNAL (all entries + RESULT stamps), RUNMAP
- Read cand_0008 diff.patch (for safe re-introduction of basic_economy error msg and multi-airport rule)
- Read cand_0009 diff.patch (for what broke: cancel return type change, broad scope lock)

## Good things to PRESERVE
- All cand_0007 improvements (route guard, max_charge, after-write reporting for flight changes, cabin dates, per-ID lookup, search before upgrade)
- All cand_0006 improvements (certificate guard, basic_economy guard, baggage no-reduce guard)
- All cand_0005 improvements (route guard, landed-flight guard, social pressure, conservative cancellation)
- All cand_0003 improvements (get_all_reservation_details, DOB logic, date-only rule, NYC mapping)
- cancel_reservation docstring: "Before calling, verify..." wording (NOT "ENFORCES")

## Deliberately skipped (cluster + why)
- Task 9 (0.50): Social pressure / over-compliance. Broad "scope lock" rules were tried (cand_0008, cand_0009) and REGRESSED passing tasks both times. The narrow approach (landed-flight guard from cand_0005) already covers the main case. Remaining flakiness is non-deterministic reasoning under flattery. Risk >> reward.
- Task 0 (0.80): Agent occasionally substitutes unrequested rebooking. Broad "only user-requested writes" rule was tried (cand_0008) and regressed tasks. At 0.80 the risk of a broad fix outweighs +0.004 potential gain.
- Task 5 (0.80): Agent over-gates delay compensation. Policy correctly says cert requires change/cancel AND user is regular with no insurance → genuinely ineligible. Flakiness is from inconsistent agent reasoning, not a fixable policy gap.
- Task 6 (0.80): Agent fabricates confirmation documents. Marginal gain (+0.004) not worth adding a new rule that could confuse other information-giving tasks.
- Task 8 (0.90): Agent uses get_all_reservation_details shortcut instead of per-ID call. Policy already mandates per-ID lookup (Mandatory Steps #7). Additional enforcement risks over-constraining other tasks.
